name: Dev
responsibility: Builds and owns all game code — engine, physics, collision, state machine, rendering, HUD, input handling. The authoritative source for what the engine can and cannot do. Defines the data formats that level and asset must conform to. Vanilla JS + HTML5 Canvas is the default stack unless the concept explicitly requires otherwise.

inputs.notes: Core loop, controls, scope constraints, known unknowns
Phase order, what's decided vs deferred
Sprite/grid dimensions needed before rendering code is written
Level data format — dev defines the schema, level populates it

outputs.description: Entry point. Game must run on localhost from this file with no build step.
All game logic — engine loop, state machine, entities, physics, input, rendering, HUD.
Data format contracts consumed by level (platform/enemy schema) and asset (sprite dimensions, file format).

merge.merge_when: Never merged — dev is always a standalone role.
merge.split_when: Engine complexity warrants splitting (e.g. separate physics engine, networking layer, or multiple platform targets).
